title: Activating New Momentum for the Conversion of Ecological Value in the Green Heart - Policy Interpretation of the "Implementation Plan for Accelerating Green Transformation and Development in the Chang-Zhu-Tan Ecological Green Heart" Part Three
description: The National Development and Reform Commission approved a Plan to accelerate the green transformation of the Chang-Zhu-Tan Ecological Green Heart, outlining tasks for ecological protection, systematic restoration, and improving environmental quality to support sustainable urban development.

analysis: The 'Implementation Plan for Accelerating the Green Transformation and Development of the Chang-Zhu-Tan Ecological Green Heart' represents a significant step towards integrating ecological sustainability with urban development. By focusing on the Green Heart's ecological security, the plan aims to enhance biodiversity and promote a resilient ecosystem that supports the surrounding urban agglomeration. The comprehensive governance strategies outlined in the plan address critical environmental issues such as air quality, soil pollution, and flood management, ensuring a holistic approach to ecological restoration. This initiative not only aims to improve the ecological environment but also seeks to enhance the quality of life for residents, making it a model for future green transformation efforts in urban areas.

key_points.content: The Plan outlines a comprehensive strategy for the green transformation of the Chang-Zhu-Tan Ecological Green Heart, integrating ecological protection with urban development.
Strengthening ecological security is a priority, with a focus on creating a resilient ecological framework that enhances biodiversity and protects vital ecosystems.
The Plan promotes sustainable development through systematic restoration and governance, ensuring the ecological environment supports the urban agglomeration's growth.

full_content.description: The National Development and Reform Commission approved a Plan to accelerate the green transformation of the Chang-Zhu-Tan Ecological Green Heart, outlining tasks for ecological protection, systematic restoration, and improving environmental quality to support sustainable urban development.

chinese_original:
近日，经国务院同意，国家发展改革委印发了《长株潭生态绿心加快绿色转型发展实施方案》（发改环资〔2025〕1751号，以下简称《方案》），提出统筹生态与安全、生态与生产、生态与生活等重点任务，明确了长株潭生态绿心（以下简称“绿心”）绿色转型发展的体制机制，为绿心推进绿色转型发展、建设都市圈美丽幸福花园提供了行动指南。
一、加快绿心绿色转型具有积极作用
绿心绿色转型对加快经济社会发展全面绿色转型、全面推进美丽中国建设具有积极作用。其一，绿心作为长沙、株洲、湘潭三个城市之间的大型生态空间，面积超过500平方公里，与周边城市共同形成紧密联动的都市圈区域，为从区域层面共保生态空间、共推绿色转型发展提供了试验地。其二，绿心各类自然生态与人文资源极其丰富，涵盖林地、水域、草地、湿地、园地等各类要素，拥有多个自然公园、景区以及多种古树名木、国家重点保护野生动物等，为山水林田湖草沙一体化保护和系统修复提供了试验田。其三，绿心生态系统服务功能突出，在水源涵养、水土保持、气候调节、固碳储碳、维护生物多样性、提供优质生态产品等方面具有重要作用，直接服务于长株潭都市圈高质量发展，为探索完善生态产品价值实现机制提供了试验场。
二、强化整体保护，筑牢都市圈生态安全格局
绿心地处都市圈中心位置，生态区位敏感，生态功能重要，在长株潭都市圈生态安全格局中具有举足轻重的地位。《方案》立足都市圈生态保护的整体性要求，通过系统构建生态格局、强化统筹保护等措施，全方位筑牢区域生态安全屏障。
一是以格局完整为目标，构建全域贯通的生态骨架。
针对以往长株潭生态绿心保护中存在的局部化、碎片化等问题，《方案》将绿心纳入长株潭都市圈生态安全框架统筹谋划，推动绿心重要生态空间的体系化保护，为都市圈生态安全提供结构性支撑。
二是以底线坚守为核心，建立分级管控的保护机制。
《方案》在严守生态保护红线的基础上，通过建立生态廊道三级管控制度，综合考虑生态区位重要程度、廊道生态条件等因素，针对不同生态廊道实行差异化的管控标准，既保障了核心生态廊道的原真性与完整性，又兼顾了生态保护与合理利用的平衡，实现了生态效益与经济效益、社会效益的有机统一。
三是以要素联通为抓手，提升生态系统的稳定性。
生态要素的跨区域流动、跨类型协同是维系生态系统结构完整、保障功能稳定的重要支撑。《方案》提出要强化山系的贯通性、水脉的连续性、蓝绿网络的关联性，加强绿心与长株潭都市圈生态系统的深度衔接和有机融合，以打造更具韧性的生态网络。
三、推进系统修复，促进绿心生态品质提升
绿心汇聚山林、草地、水体、湿地等多元自然要素。《方案》立足这一现实基础，紧扣“系统治理”思路，统筹采用自然恢复和人工修复等措施，一体推进保护修复。
一是坚持系统施治，构建山水林田湖草沙生命共同体。
生态系统的整体性决定了保护修复必须统筹兼顾、协同发力。《方案》通过统筹实施水土流失治理、废弃矿山修复、国土绿化和森林质量精准提升、水环境质量提升、全域土地综合整治等系列工程，实现各类要素生态修复、空间优化与功能提升的有机统一。
二是聚焦珍稀物种保护，完善生物多样性保护体系。
生物多样性是生态系统的核心价值所在。绿心孕育了白鹇、虎纹蛙、黑松等多种珍稀标志性动植物，是绿心生态品质的重要标尺，亟需加强保护。《方案》通过保护重要栖息地、打通迁徙廊道断点等措施，为各类生物构建起安全稳定的“生存家园”与畅通无阻的“交流通道”。
三是立足品质提升，实现生态功能与景观价值协同增效。
《方案》在强化生态保护修复的同时，注重生态功能与景观价值的统筹兼顾，通过改造林带的林分林相、提升重要风景道沿线景观、打造生态清洁小流域等，让森林、水域等生态系统既发挥固碳、涵养水源等核心生态功能，又具备观赏价值，实现“生态美”与“景观优”的有机统一。
四、坚持综合治理，改善绿心生态环境质量
绿心作为都市圈的“生态客厅”，其环境质量不仅关乎区域生态平衡与生物多样性保护，更直接影响到周边居民的宜居体验和都市圈的可持续发展。《方案》围绕气、热、土等关键环境要素，系统部署治理任务，推动生态环境质量整体提升。
一是深化大气环境治理，保卫清新蓝天。
针对都市圈产业排放、移动源污染等问题，《方案》坚持源头防控与生态净化并重，“减排”“增汇”双管齐下。一方面，以细颗粒物控制为主线，系统推进移动源治理、扬尘整治、秸秆综合利用及工业企业污染防治等，降低污染物排放。另一方面，通过推进绿心扩绿增质提高森林覆盖率，提升植被对大气污染物的吸收净化功能，构建绿色生态屏障。
二是推进通风廊道建设，营造舒爽气候。
《方案》统筹绿心与都市圈空间格局，依托湘江、浏阳河等山水生态空间，系统构建连接绿心与三市城区的通风廊道体系，增强区域小气候调节能力。通过廊道内生态修复和植被优化，提升空气流通效率，形成都市圈“自然空调”，有效缓解热岛效应，改善城乡人居环境。
三是加强土壤污染治理，守护洁净沃土。
《方案》坚持风险管控与治理修复并重，针对不同土壤污染问题精准施策。对于传统重化工业遗留土壤污染，重点推进污染地块系统治理与修复，确保土地安全再利用。针对农用地土壤安全，着力推进耕地土壤重金属污染溯源与整治，从源头保障农产品质量安全。同时，《方案》鼓励大宗固体废弃物综合利用，通过“治污”与“资源化”协同推进，实现生态效益与资源效益的双重提升。
五、聚焦风险防控，提升绿心安全韧性水平
绿心作为都市圈防洪调蓄、应急保障的核心载体，其安全韧性是区域高质量发展的重要基础。《方案》通过构建水安全、基础设施安全、应急保障一体化的韧性体系，切实增强都市圈应对自然灾害与突发公共事件的能力。
一是以流域协同为核心，建设现代化防洪排涝工程体系。
《方案》遵循“上游涵养、中游消能、下游蓄滞”的水安全格局，将绿心纳入流域生态保护治理全局，一体推进河湖生态修复、水源涵养、水土保持与洪水调蓄风险防控。充分利用绿心内季节性河道、天然蓄滞洪区、湖泊洼地等自然水体，发挥调蓄功能，同时结合水利设施建设，优化排涝布局，提升区域水安全保障能力。
二是以数字赋能为支撑，打造智慧韧性生命线系统。
《方案》结合城市更新行动和长株潭科技产业优势，重点加强燃气、供排水、桥梁、综合管廊等关键设施智能化建设，通过数字技术与生命线系统深度融合，实施智能化监测、预警和调度管理，构建互为支撑、多向联通、多源保障的韧性体系，提升基础设施系统抗风险能力。
三是以平急两用为抓手，完善公共安全应急保障体系。
《方案》按照“平急两用、城乡融合、平灾结合”思路，推进公共基础设施多功能化改造，布局一批兼具日常服务和应急保障功能的基础设施，强化绿心地区对都市圈安全韧性的支撑作用，提升区域应对突发公共安全事件的能力，保障群众生命财产安全。
《方案》通过全方位、系统性的生态保护、治理修复和品质提升措施，为加快推动绿心绿色转型筑牢了生态根基。在此基础上，《方案》还进一步提出建立生态产品价值实现机制，通过探索跨区域生态补偿、加快吸引生态环境友好型产业集聚等，不断壮大绿色发展动能，在持续做好生态“保值”的基础上，进一步做好“增值”文章。《方案》的实施，必将加快统筹各方资源、汇集各方力量，推动绿心打造成为绿色转型发展的典范样板，为加快经济社会发展全面绿色转型提供有力支撑。
（万军 生态环境部环境规划院副院长）
english_translation:
Recently, with the approval of the State Council, the National Development and Reform Commission issued the "Implementation Plan for Accelerating the Green Transformation and Development of the Chang-Zhu-Tan Ecological Green Heart" (Document No. 发改环资〔2025〕1751, hereinafter referred to as the "Plan"). The Plan proposes key tasks that coordinate ecology with safety, production, and living, clarifying the institutional mechanisms for the green transformation and development of the Chang-Zhu-Tan Ecological Green Heart (hereinafter referred to as "Green Heart"), providing an action guide for promoting green transformation and building a beautiful and happy garden in the urban agglomeration.

I. Accelerating the Green Transformation of the Green Heart Has Positive Effects
The green transformation of the Green Heart plays a positive role in accelerating the comprehensive green transformation of economic and social development and advancing the construction of a beautiful China. Firstly, as a large ecological space between the cities of Changsha, Zhuzhou, and Xiangtan, covering an area of over 500 square kilometers, the Green Heart forms a closely linked urban agglomeration area with surrounding cities, providing a testing ground for jointly protecting ecological space and promoting green transformation development at the regional level. Secondly, the Green Heart is rich in various natural ecological and cultural resources, encompassing forests, water bodies, grasslands, wetlands, and gardens, and hosts multiple natural parks, scenic spots, as well as various ancient trees and nationally protected wildlife, providing a testing ground for integrated protection and systematic restoration of mountains, rivers, forests, fields, lakes, grasslands, and deserts. Thirdly, the ecological service functions of the Green Heart are prominent, playing an important role in water source conservation, soil and water conservation, climate regulation, carbon sequestration, maintaining biodiversity, and providing high-quality ecological products, directly serving the high-quality development of the Chang-Zhu-Tan urban agglomeration and providing a testing ground for exploring and improving the mechanism for realizing the value of ecological products.

II. Strengthening Overall Protection to Build a Solid Ecological Security Pattern for the Urban Agglomeration
The Green Heart is located at the center of the urban agglomeration, with a sensitive ecological location and important ecological functions, holding a crucial position in the ecological security pattern of the Chang-Zhu-Tan urban agglomeration. The Plan is based on the overall requirements for ecological protection in the urban agglomeration, systematically constructing the ecological pattern and strengthening coordinated protection measures to build a comprehensive ecological security barrier for the region.

Firstly, aiming for a complete pattern, the Plan constructs a comprehensive ecological framework. Addressing the issues of localization and fragmentation in previous ecological protection efforts in the Green Heart, the Plan incorporates the Green Heart into the ecological security framework of the Chang-Zhu-Tan urban agglomeration for coordinated planning, promoting systematic protection of important ecological spaces in the Green Heart and providing structural support for the ecological security of the urban agglomeration.

Secondly, focusing on bottom-line adherence, the Plan establishes a graded management and control protection mechanism. Based on strict adherence to ecological protection red lines, the Plan establishes a three-tier management control system for ecological corridors, comprehensively considering factors such as the importance of ecological location and ecological conditions of the corridors, implementing differentiated management standards for different ecological corridors, ensuring the authenticity and integrity of core ecological corridors while balancing ecological protection and reasonable utilization, achieving an organic unity of ecological, economic, and social benefits.

Thirdly, leveraging element connectivity, the Plan enhances the stability of the ecosystem. The cross-regional flow and cross-type collaboration of ecological elements are important supports for maintaining the structural integrity of the ecosystem and ensuring functional stability. The Plan proposes to strengthen the connectivity of mountain systems, the continuity of water systems, and the interconnection of blue-green networks, enhancing the deep integration and organic fusion of the Green Heart with the ecological system of the Chang-Zhu-Tan urban agglomeration to create a more resilient ecological network.

III. Promoting Systematic Restoration to Enhance the Ecological Quality of the Green Heart
The Green Heart gathers diverse natural elements such as mountains, forests, grasslands, water bodies, and wetlands. Based on this reality, the Plan closely follows the idea of "systematic governance," coordinating the use of natural restoration and artificial restoration measures to promote protection and restoration in an integrated manner.

Firstly, adhering to systematic treatment, the Plan constructs a life community of mountains, rivers, forests, fields, lakes, grasslands, and deserts. The integrity of the ecosystem determines that protection and restoration must be coordinated and collaborative. The Plan implements a series of projects such as soil and water loss control, abandoned mine restoration, land greening, precise enhancement of forest quality, water environment quality improvement, and comprehensive land remediation, achieving an organic unity of ecological restoration, spatial optimization, and functional enhancement of various elements.

Secondly, focusing on the protection of rare species, the Plan improves the biodiversity protection system. Biodiversity is the core value of the ecosystem. The Green Heart nurtures various rare flagship plants and animals, such as the white pheasant, tiger-striped frog, and black pine, which are important indicators of the ecological quality of the Green Heart and require strengthened protection. The Plan constructs safe and stable "habitats" and unobstructed "communication corridors" for various organisms through measures such as protecting important habitats and breaking through migration corridor bottlenecks.

Thirdly, based on quality enhancement, the Plan achieves synergistic benefits between ecological functions and landscape values. While strengthening ecological protection and restoration, the Plan emphasizes the coordination of ecological functions and landscape values, transforming forest belts, enhancing the landscape along important scenic routes, and creating ecologically clean small watersheds, allowing ecosystems such as forests and water bodies to not only perform core ecological functions like carbon sequestration and water conservation but also possess aesthetic value, achieving an organic unity of "ecological beauty" and "landscape excellence."

IV. Adhering to Comprehensive Governance to Improve the Ecological Environment Quality of the Green Heart
As the "ecological living room" of the urban agglomeration, the environmental quality of the Green Heart not only concerns regional ecological balance and biodiversity protection but also directly affects the livability experience of surrounding residents and the sustainable development of the urban agglomeration. The Plan systematically deploys governance tasks around key environmental elements such as air, heat, and soil, promoting an overall improvement in ecological environment quality.

Firstly, deepening air environment governance to protect fresh blue skies. In response to industrial emissions and mobile source pollution in the urban agglomeration, the Plan emphasizes both source prevention and ecological purification, implementing a dual approach of "reducing emissions" and "increasing sinks." On one hand, focusing on controlling fine particulate matter, the Plan systematically promotes mobile source governance, dust control, comprehensive utilization of straw, and pollution prevention in industrial enterprises to reduce pollutant emissions. On the other hand, by promoting afforestation and quality improvement in the Green Heart to increase forest coverage, the Plan enhances vegetation's ability to absorb and purify air pollutants, constructing a green ecological barrier.

Secondly, promoting the construction of ventilation corridors to create a pleasant climate. The Plan coordinates the spatial pattern of the Green Heart and the urban agglomeration, relying on the ecological spaces of the Xiang River and Liuyang River to systematically construct a ventilation corridor system connecting the Green Heart with the urban areas of the three cities, enhancing the regional microclimate regulation capacity. Through ecological restoration and vegetation optimization within the corridors, the Plan improves air circulation efficiency, forming a "natural air conditioning" system for the urban agglomeration, effectively alleviating the heat island effect and improving the living environment in urban and rural areas.

Thirdly, strengthening soil pollution governance to protect clean arable land. The Plan emphasizes both risk control and governance restoration, implementing precise measures for different soil pollution issues. For traditional heavy chemical industry legacy soil pollution, the Plan focuses on systematic governance and restoration of polluted sites to ensure safe reuse of land. For agricultural land soil safety, the Plan emphasizes tracing and remediation of heavy metal pollution in cultivated land to ensure the quality and safety of agricultural products from the source. Additionally, the Plan encourages comprehensive utilization of bulk solid waste, promoting a dual enhancement of ecological and resource benefits through coordinated "pollution control" and "resource utilization."

V. Focusing on Risk Prevention and Control to Enhance the Safety Resilience Level of the Green Heart
As the core carrier for flood regulation and emergency support in the urban agglomeration, the safety resilience of the Green Heart is an important foundation for the region's high-quality development. The Plan constructs an integrated resilience system for water safety, infrastructure safety, and emergency support, effectively enhancing the urban agglomeration's ability to respond to natural disasters and public emergencies.

Firstly, centering on basin collaboration, the Plan builds a modern flood control and drainage engineering system. The Plan follows the water safety pattern of "upstream conservation, midstream energy dissipation, and downstream storage," incorporating the Green Heart into the overall ecological protection and governance of the basin, promoting integrated ecological restoration of rivers and lakes, water source conservation, soil and water conservation, and flood regulation risk prevention. The Plan fully utilizes seasonal rivers, natural flood storage areas, and lake depressions within the Green Heart to perform storage functions, while optimizing drainage layouts in conjunction with water conservancy facility construction to enhance regional water safety assurance capabilities.

Secondly, supported by digital empowerment, the Plan creates a smart resilience lifeline system. The Plan combines urban renewal actions with the technological advantages of the Chang-Zhu-Tan region, focusing on strengthening the intelligent construction of key facilities such as gas, water supply and drainage, bridges, and comprehensive utility tunnels. By deeply integrating digital technology with lifeline systems, the Plan implements intelligent monitoring, early warning, and dispatch management, constructing a mutually supportive, multi-directionally connected, and multi-source guaranteed resilience system to enhance the risk resistance capability of infrastructure systems.

Thirdly, leveraging both daily and emergency uses, the Plan improves the public safety emergency support system. Following the approach of "dual-use for daily and emergency, urban-rural integration, and combining peace with disaster," the Plan promotes multifunctional renovation of public infrastructure, laying out a batch of facilities that serve both daily needs and emergency support, strengthening the Green Heart's role in supporting the urban agglomeration's safety resilience, enhancing the region's ability to respond to sudden public safety incidents, and ensuring the safety of people's lives and property.

Through comprehensive and systematic ecological protection, governance restoration, and quality enhancement measures, the Plan lays a solid ecological foundation for accelerating the green transformation of the Green Heart. On this basis, the Plan further proposes establishing a mechanism for realizing the value of ecological products, exploring cross-regional ecological compensation, and accelerating the attraction of eco-friendly industries, continuously strengthening the momentum for green development, and further enhancing the "value-added" aspect while ensuring ecological "value preservation." The implementation of the Plan will undoubtedly accelerate the coordination of various resources and the gathering of various forces, promoting the Green Heart to become a model for green transformation development and providing strong support for accelerating the comprehensive green transformation of economic and social development. 
(Wan Jun, Deputy Director of the Environmental Planning Institute, Ministry of Ecology and Environment)

impact_analysis.details: The Plan's focus on ecological restoration and green transformation may drive demand for renewable energy solutions, enhancing investment in solar and wind projects.
Increased afforestation and ecosystem restoration efforts will likely boost the market for solar panels and wind turbines as part of the green infrastructure development.
The emphasis on ecological governance and pollution control will likely expand opportunities for environmental consulting and waste management services in the region.
